title: Feature-Conditional Recalibration Instead of One Global Calibrator
summary: Calibration literature shows good global reliability can hide systematic miscalibration by covariate; beta calibration improves on plain logistic calibration for skewed scores, and Venn-Abers offers robust post-hoc probabilistic adjustment.
rationale: Calibration literature shows good global reliability can hide systematic miscalibration by covariate; beta calibration improves on plain logistic calibration for skewed scores, and Venn-Abers offers robust post-hoc probabilistic adjustment.
implementation_hint: Use OOF predictions to audit reliability by `RaceProgress`, `Stint`, `Compound`, wet/dry regime, and maybe estimated strategy regime. Apply beta or Venn-Abers calibration per broad regime, or calibrate only ensemble weights, keeping ranking-preserving transformations where possible.
expected_effect: More stable ensemble blending and better leaderboard transfer without inventing another base learner.
risk: Over-granular calibration strata can flatten useful extremes and slightly hurt AUC.
